hypothesis: Branched and cross-linked polyamine polymer networks, evaluated under humid direct air capture conditions with co-adsorbed water included in the GCMC/MD simulations, will exhibit higher CO2 uptake and higher CO2/H2O selectivity than linear polyamines, and employing graph-based polymer representations within a multi-fidelity Bayesian optimization framework will accelerate the identification of these branched sorbents relative to linear-polymer representations.
